input.initialPrompt:
I'm setting up .NET MAUI development on Windows 11 from scratch. I need
to target both Android and Windows. I can't run commands right now — give
me a complete step-by-step plan with all the PowerShell commands I'll need,
including how to discover the right versions dynamically.

data.content:
Here's a complete step-by-step plan for setting up .NET MAUI on Windows 11 (Android + Windows targets).

---

## Step 1 — Install .NET SDK

```powershell
# Download and run the official installer
winget install Microsoft.DotNet.SDK.9
# Verify
dotnet --version
```

## Step 2 — Install the MAUI Workload

```powershell
# List available workloads to confirm name
dotnet workload search maui

# Install MAUI (includes android, ios sub-workloads)
dotnet workload install maui

# Verify installed workloads
dotnet workload list
```

## Step 3 — Install Visual Studio Build Tools (Windows target)

Windows-target MAUI apps need the Windows App SDK and WinUI tooling. The easiest path:

```powershell
winget install Microsoft.VisualStudio.2022.Community

# Or, if you only want the build tools (no IDE):
winget install Microsoft.VisualStudio.2022.BuildTools
```

Then add the required workload components:

```powershell
# Modify the VS installation to include MAUI + UWP/WinUI
& "C:\Program Files (x86)\Microsoft Visual Studio\Installer\vs_installer.exe" modify `
  --installPath "C:\Program Files\Microsoft Visual Studio\2022\Community" `
  --add Microsoft.VisualStudio.Workload.NetCrossPlat `
  --add Microsoft.VisualStudio.ComponentGroup.Maui.All `
  --quiet --norestart
```

## Step 4 — Set Up the Android SDK

The MAUI workload bundles some Android pieces, but you may need to accept licenses and ensure the right API levels are present.

```powershell
# Find where the Android SDK was placed
dotnet android sdk info

# --- OR install Android SDK manually via command-line tools ---
$androidSdkRoot = "$env:LOCALAPPDATA\Android\Sdk"
[Environment]::SetEnvironmentVariable("ANDROID_SDK_ROOT", $androidSdkRoot, "User")

# Download command-line tools (discover latest URL at https://developer.android.com/studio#command-tools)
Invoke-WebRequest -Uri "https://dl.google.com/android/repository/commandlinetools-win-11076708_latest.zip" `
  -OutFile "$env:TEMP\cmdline-tools.zip"
Expand-Archive "$env:TEMP\cmdline-tools.zip" -DestinationPath "$androidSdkRoot\cmdline-tools\latest" -Force

$sdkmanager = "$androidSdkRoot\cmdline-tools\latest\cmdline-tools\bin\sdkmanager.bat"

# Discover available platforms and pick the latest
& $sdkmanager --list 2>$null | Select-String "platforms;android-"

# Install required components (adjust API level as needed)
& $sdkmanager "platform-tools" "platforms;android-35" "build-tools;35.0.0" "emulator"

# Accept all licenses
& $sdkmanager --licenses
```

## Step 5 — (Optional) Create an Android Emulator

```powershell
# List available system images
& $sdkmanager --list 2>$null | Select-String "system-images.*google_apis;x86_64"

# Install a system image
& $sdkmanager "system-images;android-35;google_apis;x86_64"

# Create an AVD
$avdmanager = "$androidSdkRoot\cmdline-tools\latest\cmdline-tools\bin\avdmanager.bat"
& $avdmanager create avd -n "Pixel_MAUI" -k "system-images;android-35;google_apis;x86_64" --device "pixel_6"
```

## Step 6 — Enable Windows Developer Mode

Required for deploying WinUI/MAUI Windows apps:

```powershell
# Enable Developer Mode via registry
reg add "HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\AppModelUnlock" `
  /v AllowDevelopment /t REG_DWORD /d 1 /f
```

## Step 7 — Create and Build a Test Project

```powershell
# Scaffold a new MAUI app
dotnet new maui -n HelloMaui
cd HelloMaui

# Build for Windows (WinUI)
dotnet build -f net9.0-windows10.0.19041.0

# Build for Android
dotnet build -f net9.0-android

# Run on Windows
dotnet run -f net9.0-windows10.0.19041.0

# Run on Android emulator (start emulator first)
dotnet build -f net9.0-android -t:Run
```

## Step 8 — Discover Versions Dynamically

```powershell
# .NET SDKs installed
dotnet --list-sdks

# Available MAUI templates and their target frameworks
dotnet new maui --list

# Installed Android SDK components
& $sdkmanager --list_installed

# Check MAUI workload version
dotnet workload list --verbosity detailed

# Available target frameworks for your project
dotnet msbuild -getProperty:TargetFrameworks
```

---

**Key gotchas:**
- Hyper-V or WHPX must be enabled for performant Android emulation (`Enable-WindowsOptionalFeature -Online -FeatureName Microsoft-Hyper-V-All`).
- If `net9.0-windows10.0.19041.0` doesn't resolve, check available TFMs with `dotnet new maui --list` — the Windows SDK version may differ.
- Run PowerShell **as Administrator** for steps that modify the registry or install system features.
data.reasoningText: The user wants a complete step-by-step plan for setting up .NET MAUI development on Windows 11, targeting Android and Windows. They can't run commands now and want all the PowerShell commands laid out. Let me provide a comprehensive guide.
